title: Celebrating the Tale Annos Inspiration for Rebuild
display_title: Celebrating the Tale Annos Inspiration for Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Celebrating the Tale: Anno's Inspiration for Rebuild?             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Apr 04, 2013 9:55 pm If any of the mods feel it's best to place this in another forum, please place this thread there. Last weekend, I recieved my copy of the first US volume of Gundam: the Origin, a manga adaptation of the 1979 anime by Yoshikazu Yasuhiko, the character designer for that anime. In the back of the book, there are some collected essays about the manga, and one of them is by Anno, entitleed "Celebrating the Revival of Gundam as Tale." Here it is in it's entirety: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Apr 04, 2013 10:18 pm *feel depressed because the Italian version never included this interview* Anyway, it's a very interesting reading, even though after watching Q I'm prone to say that for better or worse Anno really out-did Yasuhiko, or better Anno's own expectations of the Origin (I stopped reading it around the death of Lalah, imo it didn't feel that much like a new story until that point).

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Apr 05, 2013 12:06 pm I'm kind of surprised that Anno still praises Gundam after Tomino bashed the hell out of Eva. Granted, he still doesn't praise Tomino specifically here, but I guess that goes to show how much of an effect the series had on him.

[]:             Quote Post by swansea » Sat Apr 06, 2013 8:58 pm Wow, I like what Anno has to say about the state of the anime industry (and I reckon it could be expanded to include mainstream entertainment in general). By and large, the material out there right now seems incredibly vapid, and it's always encouraging to hear from creative people within the industry who are willing to expose and confront that element. Certainly, I think Evangelion is a work that revels in the deconstruction and undermining of established genre tropes and it would make sense to me that Anno might have been motivated, in part, to undertake the Rebuild project in an effort to subvert audience expectation (subtly at first, gradually utilizing more deliberate tactics, and coming to a head with the massive curveball that is Q) with his own vision, thereby sort of challenging the current trends in entertainment-- or, at least, challenging people to engage something that is not among them. Jury is out on whether it can be considered any sort of celebratory success pending the completion of Final , but I think it's clear that Anno detests the shallow and the routine and hopes to make Eva something the opposite of that. He's always said the series should mean something different to everyone, after all. Also just an aside but some of his language here makes me laugh: "savor the essence and allure of Tales" "grow more fertile, more embodied"
